contraventionDescription: Parked for longer than permitted – Contravention Code 30

sections.heading: Parked for longer than permitted – Contravention Code 30
sections.content: This applies in a free parking space with a restriction on the length of stay. You must move your vehicle within the maximum time permitted.
sections.contentHtml: This applies in a free parking space with a restriction on the length of stay. You must move your vehicle within the maximum time permitted.

sections.heading: How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
sections.content: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.
sections.contentHtml: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.

sections.heading: Follow our 3 stage process:
sections.content:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.
2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.
3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.
sections.contentHtml:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.

2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.

3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.content:
Below we show diagrams of the signs which must be in situ by law and much more information that will help you decide whether to appeal against your penalty charge notice.
1. In order to be able to prove this contravention a civil enforcement officer (CEO) would need to keep meticulous notes in order to prove that the contravention occurred. It would be unlikely that you would therefore receive a parking ticket if you stayed for 25 minutes instead of twenty minutes because the parking attendant would need to make a note at the time that you arrived and as soon as the 20 minutes is up. If you make a representation along the lines that you were not parked for more than the permitted time, then the council would need to consider the notes and hand book of the parking attendant before deciding whether or not to contest
2. Normally you are not allowed to return to the same parking space for a period of time which could be an hour. This is so that valuable spaces for example outside ships are not used for commuters for example. It may be that you left the space and then returned after an hour later. How would the CEO know that you had re-parked? Well some CEO’s make notes in their pocket books or perhaps even on their hand held computer of the position of the tyre valves. They can then tell if you have re-parked your car. If you had re-parked your car you could ask to see this information as without this or regular meticulous notes it is difficult to prove.
3. Were there signs within around 15 metres of where you parked and within 5 metres of the end of the bay. Were the signs clear, pointing in the right direction and not obstructed or defaced?
4. Were the markings on the carriageway clear? Quite often they are not.
5. Please also see our section on Red Routes for information on this contravention if you received a penalty charge notice on a red route as there are many of these free bays on red routes in London. See Red Routes.
6. Please remember that you may cite compassionate grounds as a reason for asking for your PCN to be cancelled.
 
Please see our section on parking adjudicators decisions which will have details of cases for this contravention.
 
Below: Signs that should be in situ at the point of parking or within 3 car lengths of where you parked. Please note the sizes and permitted variants.
Back to Parking

Below we show diagrams of the signs which must be in situ by law and much more information that will help you decide whether to appeal against your penalty charge notice.

 

1. In order to be able to prove this contravention a civil enforcement officer (CEO) would need to keep meticulous notes in order to prove that the contravention occurred. It would be unlikely that you would therefore receive a parking ticket if you stayed for 25 minutes instead of twenty minutes because the parking attendant would need to make a note at the time that you arrived and as soon as the 20 minutes is up. 3. Were there signs within around 15 metres of where you parked and within 5 metres of the end of the bay. Were the signs clear, pointing in the right direction and not obstructed or defaced?

4. Were the markings on the carriageway clear? Quite often they are not.

5. Please also see our section on Red Routes for information on this contravention if you received a penalty charge notice on a red route as there are many of these free bays on red routes in London. See Red Routes.

6. Please remember that you may cite compassionate grounds as a reason for asking for your PCN to be cancelled.
